tenant.description: Scan your Fabric tenant — build a relationship graph from workspace(s)

--workspace.description: Workspace ID(s) or name(s) to scan (repeatable)

--deep.description: Fetch item definitions to discover embedded references (slower)

--item-types.description: Filter to specific item types (comma-separated, case-insensitive)

--no-properties.description: Skip type-specific detail fetching (fast inventory-only mode)

--format.description: Output format: graph (default) — native arrays for fabio merge/query; jsonld — instance data as RDF for triple stores; owl — OWL schema as JSON-LD for `fabio ontology import`; rdf — OWL schema as RDF/XML for `fabio ontology import` and Ontology Playground

--merge.description: Merge results into an existing graph file (incremental extraction)

--output-file.description: Write output to a file instead of stdout

--concurrency.description: Max concurrency for API calls (default: auto-scaled to CPU count)

--summary-only.description: Return only a lightweight inventory summary (item counts by type, workspace info) without building the full graph. Useful for agents to probe before a full scan

--resolve.description: Fast name-to-ID resolution. Comma-separated `Type:Name` pairs (e.g. `Notebook:my-nb,Lakehouse:bronze`). Returns matching items without full graph

--focus.description: Focus on a single item: return only the subgraph within `--depth` hops of this item ID

--depth.description: Maximum hop distance from the focused item (default: 1). Requires `--focus`

tenant.notes: Output is a graph with nodes (items), edges (relationships), workspaces metadata, summary, and a meta envelope (scannedAt, etag, scanDurationMs, partial, scope) for agent freshness tracking. Edges include confidence (high/medium/low) and discoveryMethod fields. Use --summary-only to probe workspace contents cheaply before a full scan. Use --resolve for fast name-to-ID lookups. Use --focus <id> --depth N for ego-centric subgraphs. Use --deep for full relationship discovery via definition scanning. Use --no-properties for fast inventory-only extraction. Use --output-file + --merge for incremental context building across sessions.
